parts.description:
A stepper button that decreases the field value when clicked or held.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: When `true`, the button is disabled in addition to inheriting the root's disabled state.

parts.description:
Groups the input with the increment and decrement buttons.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

The optional hidden native `input[type=number]` that mirrors the field value for browser constraint
validation (min/max/step/required) and autofill. When present it also owns native form serialization;
without it the root generates a plain hidden entry. Place it inside the root, alongside the visible
`[rdxNumberFieldInput]`.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

A stepper button that increases the field value when clicked or held.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: When `true`, the button is disabled in addition to inheriting the root's disabled state.

parts.description:
The native text input that displays the formatted value and accepts typed input.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

Groups all parts of the number field and manages its state, parsing/formatting
and value-change logic. A single directive drives the whole family — parts read
signals and call methods off it through the root context.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

inputs.name: allowOutOfRange
inputs.description:
When `true`, direct text entry may go outside the `min`/`max` range without clamping.
Step interactions (arrow keys, buttons, wheel, scrub) still clamp.

inputs.name: allowWheelScrub
inputs.description: Whether the value can be changed with the mouse wheel while the input is focused.

inputs.name: defaultValue
inputs.description: The uncontrolled value of the field when it is initially rendered.

inputs.name: disabled
inputs.description: When `true`, the user cannot interact with the field.

inputs.name: form
inputs.description: Id of an external form that owns this field.

inputs.name: format
inputs.description: Options used to format the input value (forwarded to `Intl.NumberFormat`).

inputs.name: id
inputs.description: The id of the input element.

inputs.name: largeStep
inputs.description: The step used when incrementing while the Shift key is held. Snaps to multiples of this value.

inputs.name: locale
inputs.description: The locale used to parse and format the value.

inputs.name: max
inputs.description: The maximum value of the field.

inputs.name: min
inputs.description: The minimum value of the field.

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name used when serializing this field into native `FormData`.

inputs.name: readonly
inputs.description: When `true`, the field is focusable but its value cannot be changed.

inputs.name: required
inputs.description: When `true`, the user must enter a value before the owning form can be submitted.

inputs.name: smallStep
inputs.description: The step used when incrementing while the Alt key is held. Snaps to multiples of this value.

inputs.name: snapOnStep
inputs.description: Whether the value should snap to the nearest step when incrementing or decrementing.

inputs.name: step
inputs.description: Amount to increment and decrement with the buttons, arrow keys and scrub area.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: The controlled value of the field. Use with `(onValueChange)` or two-way `[(value)]`.

inputs.name: dirty
inputs.description: Whether the value changed from its initial value. Merged with internal tracking.

inputs.name: errors
inputs.description: Validation errors for the control. A non-empty list marks it invalid.

inputs.name: invalid
inputs.description: Whether the control is invalid. A non-empty {@link errors} list also marks it invalid.

inputs.name: pending
inputs.description: Whether async validation is pending. Pending controls publish neither `data-valid` nor `data-invalid`.

inputs.name: touched
inputs.description: Whether the control has been touched. A `model()` so Signal Forms can write it; set on blur/focus-out.

outputs.name: onValueChange
outputs.description: Emitted when the value changes (during interaction or programmatically).

outputs.name: onValueCommitted
outputs.description:
Emitted when the value is committed: on blur after typing, or when a pointer is released
after scrubbing or pressing a button. Fires together with `onValueChange` for keyboard input.

outputs.name: touch
outputs.description: Emits when the control is touched, notifying Signal Forms (stable Angular 22 contract).

parts.description:
A custom element shown instead of the native cursor while scrubbing. It is portaled to the
document body and positioned with the Pointer Lock API. Hidden in Safari (which would shift
layout with the native pointer-lock notification) and for touch input.

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

An interactive area where the user can click and drag to change the field value.
Uses the Pointer Lock API for continuous dragging (disabled in Safari to avoid layout shift).

See https://base-ui.com/react/components/number-field

inputs.name: direction
inputs.description: The direction the cursor must move to change the value.

inputs.name: pixelSensitivity
inputs.description: How many pixels the cursor must move before the value changes. Higher is less sensitive.

inputs.name: teleportDistance
inputs.description: If set, the distance the cursor may move from the scrub area center before it loops back.
